title: Food-Mood Connection Awareness Check
description: This self-check helps you understand how well you currently recognize and manage the connection between what you eat and how you feel.
instructions: For each statement below, rate how true it is for you over the past 2 weeks.

questions.text: I notice how different foods affect my energy levels throughout the day
I eat regular meals rather than skipping meals or grazing all day
I can distinguish between physical hunger and emotional hunger
I include a variety of whole foods (fruits, vegetables, whole grains, protein) in my diet
I notice when my mood changes after eating certain foods
I stay adequately hydrated throughout the day
I am aware of how caffeine, sugar, and alcohol affect my mood and sleep
I make food choices based on how they make me feel, not just taste or convenience
I can manage stress without relying heavily on comfort eating
I have a balanced, non-judgmental relationship with food

bands.label: Building Awareness
bands.description: You're just starting to explore the food-mood connection. This course will help you develop foundational awareness of how nutrition affects your mental health. Small changes can make a meaningful difference.

bands.label: Developing Awareness
bands.description: You have some awareness of the food-mood connection but there's room to deepen your understanding and practice. Focus on the areas where you scored lowest — those represent your biggest opportunities for improvement.

bands.label: Good Awareness
bands.description: You have a solid foundation of food-mood awareness. This course can help you fine-tune your approach and address any remaining gaps in your nutritional wellbeing.

bands.label: Strong Awareness
bands.description: You demonstrate strong awareness of how food affects your mood and wellbeing. This course can help you maintain these healthy patterns and explore more advanced nutritional strategies for mental health.

disclaimer: This is an educational self-awareness tool, not a clinical assessment. It is designed to help you reflect on your current food-mood awareness. For personalized nutrition advice, consult a registered dietitian. If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
